  3. Seasonal calendar for microscopy specimens

Seasonal Calendar for Microscopy Specimens

The calendar indicates periods when specimens are most readily obtained in the British Isles. Many investigations remain possible outside these periods depending on local conditions.

Overview of UK Availability

Typical UK seasonal availability of specimens is outlined below:

Season What appears
Winter moulds, soil organisms, moss capsules
Early spring buds, pollen
Late spring leaf structures, insects
Summer pond life, algae
Autumn fungi, decay processes

Monthly View

Investigation Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec
Leaf epidermis       ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔      
Stomata       ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔      
Insect wing       ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔    
Pollen study     ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔            
Fern spores           ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔      
Pond microorganisms       ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔      
Bud structure   ✔ ✔ ✔                
Mould growth ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔
Decaying leaf tissue                 ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔
Moss capsules ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔ ✔           ✔ ✔

Seasonal View

Spring (March–May)

Investigation Best Month
Bud structure March–April
Pollen study April–May
Leaf epidermis April–May
Stomata May

Summer (June–August)

Investigation Best Month
Pond microorganisms June–August
Freshwater algae June–July
Insect wing June–August

Autumn (September–November)

Investigation Best Month
Fern spores September
Decaying leaf tissue October–November
Soil micro-organisms October

Winter (December–February)

Investigation Best Month
Mould growth Any month
Moss capsules January–March
